name: Dal Tadka
steps: Wash yellow lentils thoroughly and boil with turmeric and 3 cups water for 15-20 minutes until soft.
Heat ghee in a pan over medium flame. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter for 30 seconds.
Add finely chopped onions and fry until golden brown (about 5-6 minutes).
Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 2 minutes until raw smell disappears.
Add chopped tomatoes, red chilli powder and cook until oil separates (about 5 minutes).
Mash the boiled dal and pour the tadka over it. Mix well and adjust consistency with water.
Garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot with steamed rice or phulka.
tip: For extra smoky flavour, place a piece of coal in a small bowl in the dal and pour a drop of ghee over it, then cover for 2 minutes.

name: Aloo Paratha
steps: Knead wheat flour into a soft smooth dough with water, a pinch of salt and 1 tsp oil. Rest covered for 20 minutes.
Boil potatoes until completely soft. Peel and mash with no lumps.
Mix mashed potato with finely chopped onion, green chilli, cumin seeds, coriander powder, garam masala and salt.
Divide dough and filling into equal balls. Roll a dough ball into a small disc.
Place filling in centre, bring edges together and seal. Gently roll into a flat 6-inch circle.
Cook on hot tawa with butter on both sides until golden brown spots appear.
Serve hot with curd, pickle, and extra butter.
tip: Ensure no lumps in potato filling to prevent paratha from tearing while rolling.

name: Paneer Butter Masala
steps: Blanch tomatoes and blend with onions and cashews into a completely smooth paste.
Heat butter in a pan. Fry ginger-garlic paste until raw smell disappears.
Add the tomato-onion-cashew paste and cook on medium flame until butter separates (10 minutes).
Add red chilli powder, coriander powder, salt, and sugar. Cook for 5 more minutes.
Add 1/2 cup water and bring to a gentle boil. Add paneer cubes.
Stir in fresh cream and simmer for 3 minutes.
Crush kasuri methi between palms and sprinkle over gravy. Serve with naan or jeera rice.
tip: Soak cashews in warm water for 15 minutes before blending for a creamier gravy.

name: Masala Dosa
steps: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6-8 hours. Grind to smooth batter and ferment overnight.
Boil potatoes until soft. Heat oil, add mustard seeds and let them splutter.
Add curry leaves, green chilli, and sliced onions. Sauté until onions turn soft.
Add boiled mashed potatoes, turmeric, salt. Mix well to make the masala filling.
Heat a flat tawa. Sprinkle water and wipe. Pour a ladle of batter and spread in circular motion.
Drizzle oil around edges. Once golden and crispy, place potato filling in centre.
Fold dosa over filling. Serve hot with coconut chutney and sambar.
tip: The tawa must be well-seasoned and hot before spreading batter for crispy dosas.

name: Chicken Biryani
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, biryani masala, lemon juice, salt for minimum 2 hours.
Fry thinly sliced onions in ghee until deep golden brown (birista). Drain and set aside.
Parboil basmati rice with whole spices, salt until 70% cooked. Drain completely.
Cook marinated chicken in a heavy pot until 80% done. Layer cooked rice over chicken.
Add mint leaves, half the birista, saffron milk, and ghee over the rice layer.
Seal the pot tightly with dough or foil. Cook on dum on very low flame for 25 minutes.
Gently mix before serving. Garnish with remaining birista and boiled eggs. Serve with raita.
tip: The dum (slow steam) is the secret — never rush this step for perfectly cooked biryani.

name: Palak Paneer
steps: Blanch spinach in boiling salted water for 2 minutes. Immediately transfer to ice water to retain green colour.
Blend blanched spinach into completely smooth puree. Set aside.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add chopped onion and fry until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes become soft and mushy.
Add spinach puree, salt, garam masala. Simmer on medium flame for 5 minutes.
Lightly pan-fry paneer cubes until golden. Add to the spinach gravy.
Stir in cream, simmer for 2 minutes. Serve with roti or naan.
tip: Blanching spinach in ice water immediately after boiling preserves the beautiful bright green colour.

name: Chole Bhature
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight. Pressure cook with a tea bag (for dark colour) and salt for 5-6 whistles.
Prepare bhature dough with flour, yogurt, baking soda, salt. Knead well and rest for 30 minutes.
Heat oil, fry onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste, then tomatoes and chole masala.
Cook masala until oil separates. Add cooked chickpeas with some cooking water.
Simmer chole on low flame for 15 minutes until thick and well-coated.
Roll bhature dough into oval shapes. Deep fry in hot oil until puffed and golden.
Serve bhature immediately with chole, sliced onions, lemon wedge, and green chutney.
tip: Fry bhature on medium-high heat; too low makes them oily, too high burns them.

name: Egg Fried Rice
steps: Use day-old cold rice for best results. Break any clumps with a fork.
Heat oil in a wok on HIGH flame. Scramble eggs until just cooked. Remove and set aside.
In same wok, add more oil. Fry minced garlic for 30 seconds.
Add all finely chopped vegetables and stir-fry on high heat for 3 minutes.
Add rice and toss constantly on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
Add soy sauce, salt, and black pepper. Mix thoroughly.
Add scrambled eggs back, toss well. Garnish with spring onion greens. Serve immediately.
tip: High heat is the secret to non-sticky, restaurant-style fried rice.

name: Sambar
steps: Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric until very soft. Mash completely.
Soak tamarind in 1 cup warm water for 15 minutes. Squeeze and extract thick juice.
Cook drumstick pieces in tamarind water until tender.
Add sambar powder, chopped tomatoes, onion, salt and cooked dal.
Simmer on medium flame until vegetables are soft and sambar thickens (15 minutes).
Prepare tadka: heat oil, add mustard seeds, curry leaves, dried red chilli.
Pour tadka over sambar. Serve hot with idli, dosa, or steamed rice.
tip: Sambar tastes best when made a few hours ahead, allowing flavours to deepen.

name: Mango Lassi
steps: Peel ripe mangoes, remove seed, and roughly chop the flesh.
Add mango chunks, yogurt, milk, and sugar to a blender.
Blend on high speed until completely smooth.
Add cardamom powder and ice cubes. Blend for another 30 seconds.
Taste and adjust sweetness.
Pour into tall chilled glasses and serve immediately.
tip: Use Alphonso or Kesar mangoes for the best flavour and natural sweetness.

name: Vegetable Pulao
steps: Wash and soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. Drain well.
Heat oil in a heavy pot. Add whole spices and cumin seeds until fragrant.
Add sliced onion and fry until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste.
Add all chopped vegetables and sauté for 3 minutes.
Add soaked rice, 3.5 cups water, salt, and mint leaves.
Bring to a boil. Cover tightly and cook on very low flame for 15 minutes.
Switch off heat. Rest covered for 5 minutes. Fluff with fork. Serve with raita.
tip: Do not lift the lid while cooking — steam is essential for perfectly cooked pulao.

name: Gulab Jamun
steps: Make sugar syrup: boil sugar and water with cardamom pods until slightly sticky (1-string consistency). Add rose water.
Mix milk powder, all-purpose flour, baking soda, and ghee together.
Add milk gradually to make a very soft, smooth dough that is not sticky.
With greased hands, shape into small smooth balls with absolutely no cracks.
Heat oil to medium temperature (not too hot). Fry balls on LOW-MEDIUM heat, stirring gently.
Fry until dark golden brown on all sides (about 6-8 minutes).
Transfer immediately to warm sugar syrup. Soak for minimum 2 hours before serving.
tip: The key to perfect gulab jamun is frying on low heat — this ensures they cook inside without burning.

name: Butter Chicken
steps: Marinate chicken with yogurt, half the ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chilli, salt for 1 hour.
Grill or pan-fry marinated chicken until charred and cooked. Set aside.
Blend tomatoes and cashews into smooth paste.
Melt butter in pan. Fry remaining ginger-garlic paste. Add tomato-cashew paste.
Cook until butter separates. Add red chilli powder, garam masala, salt.
Add cooked chicken and cream. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Crush kasuri methi and add. Garnish with cream swirl. Serve with naan.
tip: Charring the chicken before adding to gravy gives the authentic smoky restaurant flavour.

name: Idli Sambhar
steps: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours. Grind to smooth batter and ferment overnight.
Add salt and baking soda to batter. Grease idli moulds and steam for 10-12 minutes.
For sambar: pressure cook toor dal until soft.
Prepare tamarind extract. Cook vegetables in tamarind water with sambar powder.
Add cooked dal and simmer until thick.
Prepare tadka with mustard seeds and curry leaves. Pour over sambar.
Serve hot idlis with sambar and coconut chutney.
tip: Ferment batter in a warm place for 8-12 hours for perfectly soft and fluffy idlis.

name: Rajma Chawal
steps: Soak kidney beans overnight. Pressure cook with salt for 6-8 whistles until soft.
Cook basmati rice separately. Keep warm.
Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add onions and fry until golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste. Fry for 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and all spices.
Cook masala until oil separates completely (10 minutes).
Add cooked rajma with its water. Mash some beans for thick gravy.
Simmer for 15 minutes. Serve over steamed rice with raw onion and pickles.
tip: Always use the water in which rajma was pressure cooked — it adds deep flavour to the gravy.

name: Pav Bhaji
steps: Boil potato, cauliflower, and peas together until completely soft. Mash roughly.
Heat butter in a large flat pan. Add finely chopped onions and fry until golden.
Add chopped capsicum and tomatoes. Cook until completely soft and mushy.
Add boiled mashed vegetables, pav bhaji masala, and salt. Mix and mash everything together.
Cook on medium heat, mashing constantly, adding water as needed for consistency.
Butter the pav rolls and toast on the same pan until golden and crispy.
Top bhaji with butter, lemon juice, and coriander. Serve with toasted pav and raw onions.
tip: Keep mashing the bhaji while cooking — the texture should be smooth with no whole pieces.

name: Fish Curry
steps: Marinate fish with turmeric, salt, and a pinch of red chilli for 15 minutes.
Soak tamarind in warm water. Grind onion, ginger, garlic into paste.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and curry leaves until they splutter.
Add onion-ginger-garlic paste and fry until golden (5 minutes).
Add tomatoes, red chilli powder, turmeric. Cook until oil separates.
Add tamarind extract and coconut milk. Bring to gentle boil.
Add marinated fish. Simmer gently for 8-10 minutes. Do not over-stir. Serve with rice.
tip: Gently simmer fish — boiling aggressively breaks the fish and makes it tough.

name: Chana Masala
steps: Soak chickpeas overnight. Pressure cook until very soft (4-5 whistles).
Heat oil, add cumin seeds. Add onions and fry until deep golden brown.
Add ginger-garlic paste. Fry 2 minutes. Add tomatoes and all spices.
Cook masala until oil separates well.
Add cooked chickpeas with some cooking water. Mash a few chickpeas for thick gravy.
Simmer for 10-15 minutes until flavours meld well.
Finish with lemon juice and coriander. Serve with bhature, puri, or rice.
tip: Mashing 1/4 of the chickpeas creates a naturally thick, restaurant-style gravy.

name: Upma
steps: Dry roast semolina in a pan on medium heat until light golden and aromatic (3-4 minutes). Set aside.
Heat oil, add mustard seeds and cashews. When seeds splutter, add curry leaves.
Add chopped onions, green chilli, and ginger. Sauté until onions are soft.
Add 2.5 cups boiling water and salt. Bring to a rolling boil.
Add roasted semolina gradually while stirring continuously to avoid lumps.
Cover and cook on low heat for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Switch off, add lemon juice, mix well. Garnish with coriander. Serve hot.
tip: Always add semolina to boiling water while stirring — this is the key to lump-free upma.

name: Kheer
steps: Wash rice. Soak saffron in 2 tbsp warm milk.
Bring full-fat milk to boil in a heavy-bottomed pot. Reduce to medium flame.
Add washed rice to boiling milk. Cook stirring frequently for 30-35 minutes.
Milk will reduce to half. Rice should be completely soft and dissolved.
Add sugar, crushed cardamom, and saffron milk. Stir well.
Add sliced almonds, cashews, and raisins. Cook 5 more minutes.
Add rose water. Serve warm or chilled, garnished with chopped nuts.
tip: Use full-fat milk and keep stirring frequently to prevent burning at the bottom.
